new_vs_pre_existing_mechanism.description: Inspector emits findings for files listed in --changed-files and tags each finding with explicit boolean field 'introduced_in_diff'. close.md uses this tag per AC-2.6(b) NEW-positive marker rule. An absent/null tag would be treated as pre-existing/advisory per default-safe ignore rule, so every finding below carries the field explicitly.
new_vs_pre_existing_mechanism.diff_source: git diff HEAD against the working tree at task-id 20260520-221452 commit prep stage

findings.description: The newly added Standard 6 'Exemption (Cycle-2 user-facing source-language prompt strings)' paragraph is a single ~1700-character / 18-line wall paragraph stuffed with four enumerated sub-clauses (i)/(ii)/(iii)/(iv), a re-affirmation clause, authoring-cycle attribution, and a codex consultation disposition stamp. Two problems: (1) RULES-NOT-STORIES: the trailing 'Authoring cycle: task-id 20260520-221452 (Cycle 2 of spec-20260518-225715). Codex consultation findings #10 + iter-2 #4 disposition: applied.' is narrative provenance metadata, not an execution rule that style-inspector reads to decide a violation. The pre-existing exemption paragraph above (verbatim user-binding quotes) carries a similar 'Authoring cycle: ... Precipitating failure: ...' tail and establishes a precedent, but BOTH paragraphs would be cleaner without the provenance — the precedent does not make this introduction non-verbose, it makes it consistent verbosity. (2) READABILITY: the four exempted classes would be substantially more scannable as a bulleted list (one bullet per class) rather than one sentence with parenthetical enumeration markers. Recommendation: convert the four exempted classes to a bulleted list under a 2-sentence intro, and move the provenance line to a single 'Provenance:' suffix (or drop it — the git history carries this).

Three newly added sentences are pure historical narrative about the file's own refactor, not execution rules. Line 826: 'This pre-decision-tree pass — formerly labelled with a decimal sub-step (eliminated per spec-20260518-225715 Cycle 2 P3.7 / /dev integer-step-numbering Standard 4) — applies QA's reported spec section updates ...'. Line 867: 'This post-decision-tree pass — formerly labelled with a decimal sub-step (eliminated per spec-20260518-225715 Cycle 2 P3.7 / /dev integer-step-numbering Standard 4) — applies the canonical score-update events ... It stays inside Step 12 (the decimal was the only thing removed; ordering, semantics, and trigger gates are unchanged).'. Line 1118: 'Summarise score-update events from Step 12 (the score-update events pass, formerly labelled with a decimal sub-step — see spec-20260518-225715 Cycle 2 P3.7) and any user-rating events ...'. The 'formerly labelled with a decimal sub-step (eliminated per ...)' clauses describe HISTORY of the prompt file's own edits — they tell a STORY about what was renamed, they do NOT tell the orchestrator what to do. The orchestrator does not need to know what the section used to be called; the spec file already records that. Recommendation: drop the 'formerly labelled / eliminated per ...' parenthetical from all three sites. The intro sentence then reads cleanly as a rule: 'Apply spec section updates (before the decision tree). If a Spec file was non-null this cycle ...' — semantics preserved, story removed. The spec-20260518-225715 §5.1 / Cycle 2 attribution lives in git blame and in the spec file; it does not belong in the execution prompt.

The new 4-line preamble before the Output JSON Report code-block restates the same information that Procedure step 5 (rewritten in the same diff at lines 80-89) already conveys: 'manifest_path is the per-task active manifest path; the global index file at tests/generated/manifest.json (shape {kind: "index", tasks: [...]} — i.e. kind: index, NOT the active manifest) is updated as a side effect of Procedure step 5 and is NOT this field's value'. Step 5 already states the same per-task-vs-index split with the same {kind: index, tasks: [...]} shape annotation. The Output JSON block fields themselves (manifest_path, global_index_path, global_index_kind) carry the names; the preamble is mostly redundant restatement. Recommendation: compress to one sentence: 'manifest_path is the per-task active manifest; global_index_path is the {kind: index} side-effect upsert from Procedure step 5.' — drops ~3 lines while keeping the disambiguation.

Phase 5 Step 1 is now a single ~5-line sentence with a long parenthetical disambiguation: 'Read the per-task active manifest at tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json (this is the file that carries active_tests[]; the global file tests/generated/manifest.json is a thin {kind: "index", tasks: [...]} index and MUST NOT be consulted for active_tests[]). For every active_tests[] entry, verify the test file exists and is importable: ( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 -c ... )'. Step 5 similarly has a long parenthetical 'sentinel: tests/generated/manifest.json is missing — global index absence is the canonical sentinel, NOT the absence of an active_tests[] array inside it' — note this prose carries an explicit verbatim sentinel-token contract that close.md/QA depends on, so the prose itself is FUNCTIONAL (not story); BUT the wrapping sentence structure makes the rule hard to parse on a fast read. The new manifest_verification schema _doc string (line 1434) crosses 350 chars with two semicolon-joined clauses about kind=index and 'NOT this field's manifest_path'. None of this content is non-functional — it is all RULES — but the parenthetical density makes the rules expensive to extract. Recommendation: split each multi-clause sentence into a leading rule sentence followed by a 1-line parenthetical clarifier on its own line. No semantic change; faster human + agent comprehension. Severity is minor because the content is functional, not story.
findings.verbose_lines_note: no lines are pure verbosity; readability rather than length is the concern

findings.description: Three dispatch-prompt lines now carry long inline parenthetical cross-references to qa.md Phase 5: Step 11 test-writer dispatch (lines 635-636): 'Per-task active manifest: tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json (active_tests[] lives here — this is what QA Phase 5 reads). / Global index: tests/generated/manifest.json (index) (shape {kind:"index", tasks:[{task_id, manifest_path}]}; upsert an entry for the current task_id — does NOT carry active_tests[]).'. Step 11 Dev dispatch (line 666): 'Generated tests (when test-writer ran): tests/generated/<task_id>/ + per-task active manifest at tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json (the global file tests/generated/manifest.json (index) is consulted only as a presence sentinel for cross-task discovery — its shape is {kind:"index", tasks:[...]} and it does NOT carry active_tests[])'. Step 11 QA dispatch (line 808): 'Per-task active test manifest: tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json (the file QA Phase 5 reads active_tests[] from; the global file tests/generated/manifest.json (index) is only consulted as a presence sentinel — see agents/qa.md Phase 5)'. Each repeats the same {kind:index, tasks:[...]} shape annotation and presence-sentinel framing. These are dispatch prompts (already long); the cross-reference to qa.md Phase 5 is sufficient — the shape annotation does not need to be re-rendered three times. Recommendation: state the per-task path on its own line; defer the shape annotation to qa.md Phase 5 + agents/test-writer.md Procedure step 5 (where it already lives in canonical form). Drop the parenthetical redefinitions from the three dispatch sites. Saves ~5 lines across commands/dev.md, no semantic loss because canonical definition sites remain.

non_findings.description: Spec narrative file. Section 2 (What Was Attempted) Cycle 2 narrative + Section 3 (What Was Changed) Cycle 2 file-list are EXACTLY the artefact's purpose — spec files document what happened per cycle. The 'rules not stories' principle applies to execution prompts (agents/*.md, commands/*.md), not to specification narratives. The added 13 sub-sections + 28 file-entries in Section 3 are appropriate for a spec record. No finding.

Per dispatch: auto-generated by hooks/doc_sync/regen_readme.py + tree.py. Treated as advisory. The diff removes 354 lines (3 leaked agent-scores listing entries + 2 description-string rewordings) — net REDUCTION in verbosity, not increase. No finding.
Per dispatch: auto-generated, advisory. Diff removes 2 leaked agent-scores listing entries — net REDUCTION. No finding.

summary.verdict_rationale: No critical 'rules not stories' violations and no critical verbosity scores (no file added >=100 verbose lines). The two major findings (style-inspector.md exemption paragraph density, commands/dev.md historical-narrative sub-step justifications) are recommended cleanups but do not represent catastrophic prompt bloat. The three minor findings are readability/redundancy concerns. Recommendation: address the two major findings in this cycle if possible (small, low-risk edits); minor findings can defer. Per the prompt-inspector default-safe contract documented in the agent prompt: an absent positive marker on a file-level finding is treated as pre-existing/advisory by close.md — every finding here carries introduced_in_diff: true so close.md may correctly classify them as NEW per AC-2.6(b).
